title: chaenorhinum minus subsp. anatolicum
intro: Chaenorhinum minus subsp. anatolicum, also known as Turkish catmint, is a subspecies of the Chaenorhinum minus plant, a type of flowering plant in the phrymaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Turkish catmint can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
